openFindings.title: deriveStatus false-complete is a systemic repo-wide tooling defect: 363 actually-incomplete folders mislabeled 'complete' by a file-presence-only heuristic; 213 already live-corrupted on-disk today
evidenceRefs: graph-metadata-parser.ts:1215-1218 (deriveStatus: implementation-summary.md present + no checklist.md => 'complete', unconditional)
repo-wide sweep: 363 folders would be mislabeled; 213 already carry a false derived.status:'complete' on disk today

iteration 6: exhaustive consumer grep shows resume ladder / phase-map sync / completion verification all read other fields (last_active_child_id, canonical-doc frontmatter), not derived.status -- currently inert, not a live functional failure
iteration 9: no validator in the validate.sh --strict chain cross-checks status vs completion_pct/open-tasks (sibling finding D1-P1-001)
openFindings.counterevidenceSought: Searched for any consumer that branches on derived.status (iter 6); searched for a cross-field validator that would already catch this (iter 9); both searches came back empty, strengthening rather than refuting the finding
openFindings.alternativeExplanation: The 213 on-disk rows could be genuinely complete with stale/missing completion_pct rather than falsely derived -- ruled out as the dominant case: iteration 5/9 cross-checked against completion_pct/open-tasks and found the false-complete population, though a null-completion_pct subset (296 folders) has residual ambiguity requiring per-folder review before an automated fix executes broadly
openFindings.downgradeTrigger: A deriveStatus patch ships gating on completion_pct/open-tasks, OR a per-folder authoring audit shows the 213 rows are genuinely complete with stale pct -- neither has happened; adjudication (iteration 10) HELD P0

openFindings.title: Validation layer has no cross-field consistency check between derived.status and canonical completion signals (completion_pct, open tasks, checklist content); validate.sh --strict is structurally blind to false-complete
evidenceRefs: generated-metadata-integrity.ts:~99 (enum-membership check on derived.status only, no cross-field check)
generated-metadata-drift.ts:224-242 (re-derives description/causal_summary, never re-derives or checks status; additionally tautological since it would reuse the same buggy deriveStatus)
continuity-freshness validator: within-canonical-doc only, does not cross into graph-metadata.json
5 validators in the validate.sh --strict chain reviewed; none joins derived.status to completion_pct/open-tasks/checklist.md content
openFindings.counterevidenceSought: Checked whether the grandfather rollout mechanism (isGeneratedMetadataGrandfatherEnabled) could cover this gap by design -- confirmed it applies uniformly to schema-level violations only, not a documented substitute for a cross-field status check
openFindings.alternativeExplanation: This could be an intentional design choice (status is a derived/advisory field never meant to be strictly validated) rather than a gap -- weighed and rejected in iteration 9: nothing in the codebase documents this as an intentional non-contractual field, and it is the structural reason a confirmed P0-magnitude defect (D4-P0-001) passes --strict undetected
openFindings.downgradeTrigger: Documentation surfaces establishing derived.status as explicitly non-contractual/advisory-only, OR the defect stops mattering because D4-P0-001 is fixed at the producer -- neither has happened; adjudication (iteration 10) HELD P1 (structural enabler of a latent P0, not itself producing wrong data)

openFindings.title: Loader/validator read-path inconsistency: the runtime graph-metadata loader tolerates and auto-migrates legacy plain-text scaffold content; the strict validator does a raw JSON.parse with no such tolerance
evidenceRefs: graph-metadata-parser.ts:356-433 (validateGraphMetadataContent -> parseLegacyGraphMetadataContent tolerant fallback, migrated:true)
generated-metadata-integrity.ts:69-92 (readJsonFile does a raw JSON.parse with no legacy fallback)

backfill-graph-metadata.js --dry-run against phase-009 returns existing:1, refreshed:1, failed:[] (loader succeeds); tsx generated-metadata-integrity.ts --strict on the same folder returns FILE_UNPARSEABLE (validator fails)
openFindings.counterevidenceSought: Checked whether the grandfather flag selectively carves out FILE_UNPARSEABLE -- confirmed it does not (uniform), so this is a genuine, not incidental, split
openFindings.alternativeExplanation: Could be an intentional one-way migration gate (legacy files load at runtime but must be regenerated to pass strict validation) rather than a defect -- plausible reading, recorded as the leading interpretation alongside the inconsistency framing
openFindings.downgradeTrigger: A second unparseable file is found elsewhere (would escalate), or the runtime acts on stale derived fields of migrated metadata in a way that causes real harm

openFindings.title: create_graph_metadata_file()'s early-return guard prevents any scaffolding re-run from self-healing a malformed legacy graph-metadata.json
evidenceRefs: create.sh:408-410 (if [[ -f "$graph_path" ]]; then return 0; fi)
create.sh:452-479 (current heredoc emits valid schema-conformant JSON when no file exists)
sibling 010-security-and-correctness-fixes/graph-metadata.json is valid, confirming the current tool is not itself buggy in normal operation
openFindings.counterevidenceSought: n/a -- structural guard behavior confirmed by direct code read, not in dispute
openFindings.alternativeExplanation: The guard is deliberate (never clobber operator/manual edits) -- reasonable design intent, but it means there is no automated self-heal path for this class of legacy file; an explicit repair script/flag is required

openFindings.title: Seeded Known Context arithmetic error self-corrected: the mtime-delta claim of '~53 minutes' between phase-009 scaffold files was actually ~4 hours 53 minutes
openFindings.scope: Review-process accuracy correction, not a phase-009/tooling defect
evidenceRefs: stat mtimes: spec/plan/tasks/impl-summary = 06:47:26; graph-metadata.json = 11:40:36 (+0200)

openFindings.title: Seeded Known Context count error self-corrected: git stash list has 6 entries, not the 5 originally cited

The 'owned by a separate, concurrently in-flight OpenCode session' ownership claim is asserted across ~35 packet-032 docs with no cited verification method; refined across iterations 2/7/8 into a split verdict (past-tense session existence substantiated on commit prose at ~0.80 confidence after correcting an over-read of a commit trailer; present-tense active editing and phase-009-specific attribution remain unverified)
evidenceRefs: packet-root spec.md, changelog-032-root.md, and ~33 other packet-032 docs repeat the claim without citing a verification method (iteration 2)
commit 8405ba4f57 author-message prose: 'already renamed in the shared working tree, uncommitted ... matching what a concurrent session had independently converged on' (iteration 7)
iteration 8: the commit's Claude-Session trailer is the authoring/committing session's own stamp, not corroboration of a distinct concurrent session -- downgrades iteration 7's 0.90 to 0.80

base-rate comparator (design-039 packet) shows this repo's genuine concurrent-session claims carry named files + line-count fingerprints + captured concurrency-state; phase 009's claim carries none of these
openFindings.counterevidenceSought: Repo-wide git reflog/stash/lock-file sweep (iterations 2, 5, 7) found zero local corroboration; explicitly weighed the counter-consideration that a remote/detached session would be invisible to local git/lock state
openFindings.alternativeExplanation: The claim is an operator reservation/label for future work rather than a statement about an active peer session right now -- consistent with handover.md's own future-tense framing ('to next OpenCode implementation session')
openFindings.downgradeTrigger: n/a -- already the settled, refined verdict as of iteration 8

openFindings.title: Rename-citation inaccuracy: packet docs cite the concurrent-session rename as 'opencode_goal.md -> goal_opencode.md', but 'opencode_goal.md' was never a committed git path -- the actual prior committed name was 'goal.md'

Claude-Session / Co-Authored-By commit trailers are committer/model attribution only; they cannot corroborate a distinct concurrent session because a working-tree-only (never-committed) session leaves no trailer by definition -- corrects iteration 7's treatment of the trailer as 'a concrete handle'
evidenceRefs: git log --all -S 'session_01MaduKvU39V7TZ4qrdB8b5k' --pickaxe-regex: 16 of 588 commits, one coherent time-bounded burst -- a real per-session id, not fixed template text (refutes the reviewer's own alternative 'fixed template' hypothesis too)
Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 5 appears on 26 commits repo-wide -- model-level, not session-level, attribution

openFindings.title: Phase-count contradiction: phase 009's own spec.md says 'Phase 9 of 9' while the packet-root phase map enumerates 14 phases with 010-014 already Complete

No concrete scope statement exists in any canonical doc for phase 009 -- both its own spec.md ('[To be defined during planning]') and the packet-root phase map ('[Phase 9 scope]') are placeholders; handover.md is the sole real source of scope

A live graph-metadata regeneration would also regress derived synopsis/importance_tier (important -> normal) and replace the synopsis with spec.md placeholder text, because the source docs are unauthored scaffolds -- an accuracy regression distinct from the P0 status defect

note: Regenerated 2026-07-02 after the shared reduce-state.cjs reducer degraded this file (a findingsNew shape mismatch produced 3 generic 'SUMMARY-P2-*' placeholders and dropped the P0/P1 and 9 of 10 P2 findings). Restored from deep-review-state.jsonl (12 lines, intact) and deep-review-strategy.md (intact) plus all 10 iteration-NNN.md files. The reduce-state.cjs schema mismatch is a process-tooling observation for this orchestration run, not a phase-009 finding.
